concepts.name: Spectral triple
concepts.definition: A triple (A, H, D) of a *-algebra A represented as bounded operators on a Hilbert space H together with a self-adjoint Dirac operator D with compact resolvent such that [D, a] is bounded for all a in A. It is the noncommutative generalization of a smooth compact Riemannian spin manifold; all geometric information is encoded in D.

concepts.name: Dirac operator (spectral geometry)
concepts.definition: Self-adjoint unbounded operator D with compact resolvent that encodes the metric, dimension and differential structure of a noncommutative space; its spectrum determines the geometry and the spectral action depends only on it.

concepts.name: Real structure (charge conjugation J)
concepts.definition: Antiunitary operator J on H satisfying J^2 = epsilon, JD = epsilon' DJ, J gamma = epsilon'' gamma J, with signs fixed by the KO-dimension mod 8. It makes the spectral triple 'real', encodes charge conjugation, and the choice KO-dim 6 mod 8 resolves fermion doubling in the Standard Model.

concepts.name: KO-dimension
concepts.definition: The dimension modulo 8 (from Bott periodicity of real Clifford algebras) determining the sign table (epsilon, epsilon', epsilon'') of the real structure J. The finite Standard Model space has KO-dimension 6 mod 8; total spacetime is 4 + 6 = 10 = 2 mod 8.

concepts.name: Connes distance formula
concepts.definition: Metric reconstructed from the Dirac operator as d(phi, psi) = sup{ |phi(a) - psi(a)| : ||[D, a]|| <= 1 }. Recovers geodesic distance for pure states on a manifold and the order-1 Wasserstein (optimal transport) distance for mixed states. The metric is derived, not postulated.

concepts.name: Reconstruction theorem
concepts.definition: Connes' theorem (2008): a commutative spectral triple satisfying seven axioms (dimension, regularity, finiteness, reality, first-order, orientability, Poincare duality) necessarily arises from a compact oriented Riemannian spin manifold. Establishes the equivalence geometry = algebra + Dirac operator.

concepts.name: First-order condition
concepts.definition: Axiom [[D, a], J b* J^{-1}] = 0 stating that D acts as a first-order differential operator. Relaxing it (post-2013) yields Pati-Salam extensions and inner fluctuations beyond the Standard Model.

concepts.name: Spectral action principle
concepts.definition: Action principle S = Tr f(D/Lambda) + <psi, D psi> asserting that all physics depends only on the spectrum of the Dirac operator. Its heat-kernel expansion yields gravity (cosmological constant, Einstein-Hilbert, Weyl) plus Yang-Mills and Higgs sectors of the Standard Model from a single functional.

concepts.name: Heat-kernel (Seeley-DeWitt) expansion
concepts.definition: Asymptotic expansion Tr f(D/Lambda) ~ f_4 Lambda^4 a_0 + f_2 Lambda^2 a_2 + f_0 a_4 + O(Lambda^-2) in terms of Seeley-DeWitt coefficients a_k of D^2; produces the cosmological constant (Lambda^4), the Einstein-Hilbert term (Lambda^2, Newton constant), and the Weyl-squared term (Lambda^0).

concepts.name: Almost-commutative geometry
concepts.definition: Product of a continuous 4-manifold M with a finite noncommutative space F: A = C^infinity(M) tensor A_F. The continuous factor encodes gravity; the finite factor encodes internal gauge degrees of freedom of particle physics.

concepts.name: Finite Standard Model algebra
concepts.definition: The finite algebra A_F = C + H + M_3(C) (complex numbers, quaternions, 3x3 matrices) whose unitary group, after unimodular reduction, yields the gauge group U(1) x SU(2) x SU(3) of the Standard Model.

concepts.name: Inner fluctuations of the metric
concepts.definition: Fluctuations D -> D_A = D + A + epsilon' J A J^{-1} with A = sum a_i [D, b_i], generated by inner automorphisms of the algebra. The continuous part gives gauge fields; the discrete part gives the Higgs field.

concepts.name: Higgs field as a connection
concepts.definition: In almost-commutative geometry the Higgs field arises from off-diagonal components of the finite Dirac operator, interpreted as parallel transport in the discrete internal direction; its quartic potential is fixed by the spectral action.

concepts.name: Standard Model from spectral geometry
concepts.definition: Derivation of the full Standard Model (gauge group, fermion representations, hypercharges, Higgs, neutrino mixing) minimally coupled to gravity from the spectral action on an almost-commutative geometry with finite algebra C + H + M_3(C).

concepts.name: Fermion doubling problem
concepts.definition: Spurious multiplication of fermionic degrees of freedom in the noncommutative Standard Model, resolved by choosing KO-dimension 6 mod 8 for the finite space and using the real structure J to enforce correct charge-conjugate pairing.

concepts.name: Quanta of geometry (volume quantization)
concepts.definition: A higher-degree Heisenberg relation involving D and Feynman-slashed coordinates Y (Y^2=1) implies quantization of the volume in Planck units; in dimension 4 the two-sided version forces the algebras M_2(H) and M_4(C) underlying the Standard Model.

concepts.name: Noncommutative spacetime
concepts.definition: Spacetime whose coordinates are operators with nonzero commutators: canonical/Moyal [x^mu, x^nu] = i theta^{mu nu} or Lie-algebra type kappa-Minkowski [x^0, x^i] = (i/kappa) x^i, implying minimal length and modified dispersion relations.

concepts.name: Moyal star product
concepts.definition: Noncommutative product (f star g)(x) = exp((i/2) theta^{mu nu} d_mu^x d_nu^y) f(x) g(y)|_{y=x} replacing pointwise multiplication; encodes canonical noncommutativity at the level of the function algebra. Appears in D-brane effective theories.

concepts.name: kappa-Minkowski spacetime
concepts.definition: Lie-algebra noncommutative spacetime with [x^0, x^i] = (i/kappa) x^i, [x^i, x^j] = 0, where kappa is a Planck-energy scale. It is dual to the momentum sector of the kappa-Poincare Hopf algebra and underlies many DSR models.

concepts.name: kappa-Poincare Hopf algebra
concepts.definition: Quantum (Hopf-algebra) deformation of the Poincare algebra that is the symmetry of kappa-Minkowski spacetime; its bicrossproduct basis yields deformed boosts and an energy-dependent speed of light, reducing to ordinary Poincare as kappa -> infinity.

concepts.name: Doubly/deformed special relativity (DSR)
concepts.definition: Relativistic framework with two observer-independent scales: the speed of light c and a Planck length/energy scale. Realized via kappa-Poincare; predicts deformed dispersion relations and energy-dependent photon velocity testable with gamma-ray bursts.

concepts.name: Relative locality
concepts.definition: Principle that locality of events becomes observer-dependent when momentum space is curved/noncommutative; a refinement of DSR in which coincidence of events at a distance is not absolute.

concepts.name: Minimal length
concepts.definition: A fundamental, observer-independent minimal measurable length, typically of order the Planck length, predicted by string theory, LQG, noncommutative geometry, DSR and black-hole physics. Implemented via the generalized uncertainty principle.

concepts.name: Generalized uncertainty principle (GUP)
concepts.definition: Modified commutator [x, p] = i hbar (1 + beta p^2) leading to Delta x Delta p >= (hbar/2)(1 + beta (Delta p)^2 + ...) and a minimal position uncertainty Delta x_min = hbar sqrt(beta). A model-independent phenomenological signature of quantum gravity.

concepts.name: Doplicher-Fredenhagen-Roberts quantum spacetime
concepts.definition: Model deriving spacetime noncommutativity [x^mu, x^nu] = i ell_P^2 Q^{mu nu} from the requirement that localizing an event better than the Planck length would create a black hole, combining the Heisenberg principle with classical gravity.

concepts.name: Fuzzy spaces
concepts.definition: Finite matrix approximations of manifolds (e.g. fuzzy sphere S^2_N) whose function algebra is M_N(C); spherical harmonics are truncated at a maximal spin. They are finite spectral triples with matrix Dirac operators and converge to the continuum as N -> infinity.

concepts.name: Fuzzy sphere
concepts.definition: Canonical noncommutative geometry approximating S^2: algebra M_N(C) generated by truncated spherical harmonics with [x_i, x_j] = i (theta) epsilon_{ijk} x_k; recovers the round 2-sphere as N -> infinity.

concepts.name: Random noncommutative geometry (Dirac ensembles)
concepts.definition: Path-integral quantization over fluctuating finite Dirac operators on finite real spectral triples (fuzzy geometries), a noncommutative analogue of integration over metrics. Reduces to multimatrix integrals; studied by Barrett-Glaser numerically and analytically.

concepts.name: Matrix models (IKKT/BFSS, multimatrix)
concepts.definition: Models whose dynamical variables are large matrices; fuzzy spaces and noncommutative gauge theories emerge from them, and effective Riemannian geometry/gravity emerges from matrix fluctuations. Multimatrix integrals arise from Dirac ensembles.

concepts.name: Spectral Pati-Salam model
concepts.definition: Beyond-Standard-Model unification SU(2)_L x SU(2)_R x SU(4) obtained by relaxing the first-order condition in the spectral framework, exhibiting grand unification of the Pati-Salam gauge couplings.

concepts.name: Seiberg-Witten map
concepts.definition: Explicit field redefinition mapping a commutative gauge theory to an equivalent noncommutative (Moyal) one; underlies the equivalence of commutative and noncommutative descriptions of D-branes in a constant B-field.

concepts.name: Noncommutative quantum field theory
concepts.definition: QFT on noncommutative spacetime (Moyal or kappa-Minkowski) using star products; exhibits UV/IR mixing, modified propagators and, for kappa-Minkowski, nontrivial coproducts requiring braided/Hopf-algebraic treatment.

concepts.name: Spectral dimension
concepts.definition: Effective dimension probed by diffusion/heat-kernel return probability. In several deformed noncommutative models (and in asymptotic safety, CDT, LQG) it runs from 4 in the IR toward 2 in the UV ('dimensional reduction'), depending on the choice of deformed Laplacian.

concepts.name: UV dimensional reduction
concepts.definition: Phenomenon, shared across many quantum-gravity approaches, in which the effective (spectral) dimension of spacetime drops to about 2 at short distances. In NCG it is sensitive to the deformed Laplacian/star product chosen.

formulas.name: Spectral triple conditions
formulas.meaning: Defines a spectral triple: bounded commutators [D,a] make algebra elements Lipschitz functions; compact resolvent of D encodes compactness and discrete spectrum of the space. All geometry sits in D.

formulas.name: Real structure sign relations
formulas.meaning: Antiunitary J obeys three sign relations with epsilon, epsilon', epsilon'' in {+1,-1} fixed by KO-dimension mod 8 via Bott periodicity. KO-dim 6 for the SM finite space resolves fermion doubling.

formulas.name: First-order condition
formulas.meaning: States that D is a first-order differential operator. Its relaxation generates extra inner fluctuations and the Pati-Salam extension beyond the Standard Model.

formulas.name: Connes distance formula
formulas.meaning: Reconstructs the metric from D as a supremum over algebra elements; gives geodesic distance for pure states and order-1 Wasserstein distance for mixed states. Distance is derived, not assumed.

formulas.name: Spectral action principle
formulas.meaning: Bosonic part depends only on the spectrum of D (cutoff function f, scale Lambda); fermionic part is the Dirac action. This single functional yields gravity plus the full Standard Model.

formulas.name: Heat-kernel expansion of the spectral action
formulas.meaning: Asymptotic expansion in moments f_k of f and Seeley-DeWitt coefficients a_k of D^2; produces cosmological constant (Lambda^4), Einstein-Hilbert/Newton constant (Lambda^2) and Weyl-squared (Lambda^0) terms.

formulas.name: Emergent gravitational action
formulas.meaning: The geometric part of the spectral action: cosmological term, Einstein-Hilbert term with Newton constant G ~ 1/(f_2 Lambda^2), and conformal Weyl-squared curvature term. Gravity emerges spectrally.

formulas.name: Inner fluctuations of the Dirac operator
formulas.latex: D_A=D+A+\varepsilon' J A J^{-1},\qquad A=\sum_i a_i\,[D,b_i]
formulas.meaning: Fluctuated Dirac operator from inner automorphisms; the continuous part of A gives gauge fields, the discrete part the Higgs field, and JAJ^{-1} enforces correct charge conjugation.

formulas.name: Higher Heisenberg (volume quantization) relation
formulas.meaning: With Y a Feynman-slashed coordinate and n commutators, this relation quantizes the volume in Planck units and in dimension 4 forces the algebras M_2(H) and M_4(C) of the Standard Model.

formulas.name: Canonical (Moyal) noncommutativity
formulas.meaning: Constant antisymmetric theta^{mu nu} (dimension length^2) defines canonical noncommutative spacetime; appears in D-brane effective theories with theta ~ B^{-1}.

formulas.name: kappa-Minkowski commutator
formulas.meaning: Lie-algebra-type noncommutativity with Planck-energy scale kappa; breaks boosts but preserves rotations, is dual to the kappa-Poincare Hopf algebra and underlies DSR.

formulas.name: Moyal star product
formulas.meaning: Deformed product encoding noncommutativity on the algebra of ordinary functions; to first order f star g = fg + (i/2) theta^{mu nu} d_mu f d_nu g.

formulas.name: Generalized uncertainty principle
formulas.meaning: Kempf-Mangano-Mann deformed commutator yields a nonzero minimal position uncertainty of order the Planck length; a model-independent quantum-gravity signature.

formulas.name: DFR spacetime uncertainty relations
formulas.latex: \Delta x^{0}\big(\Delta x^{1}+\Delta x^{2}+\Delta x^{3}\big)\gtrsim\ell_P^2,\qquad \sum_{i<j}\Delta x^{i}\Delta x^{j}\gtrsim\ell_P^2
formulas.meaning: Doplicher-Fredenhagen-Roberts relations following from [x^mu,x^nu]=i ell_P^2 Q^{mu nu}; derived from the requirement that Planckian localization not form a black hole.

formulas.name: Fuzzy sphere coordinate algebra
formulas.meaning: Noncommutative coordinates of the fuzzy sphere proportional to su(2) generators of spin j; algebra is M_N(C) with N=2j+1, recovering the round sphere as j -> infinity.

references.title: Quantized Space-Time
references.significance: First model of noncommutative spacetime; anticipated NCG by decades.

references.authors: S. Doplicher, K. Fredenhagen, J. E. Roberts
references.title: The quantum structure of spacetime at the Planck scale and quantum fields
references.significance: Derives spacetime noncommutativity from quantum mechanics plus classical gravity (black-hole localization bound).

references.title: Noncommutative Geometry
references.significance: Founding monograph; axioms of the spectral triple.

references.title: Noncommutative geometry and reality
references.significance: Introduces real structure J and KO-dimension, central to the SM construction.

references.title: The Spectral Action Principle
references.significance: Foundational: physics depends only on the spectrum of D; yields gravity plus gauge theory.

references.authors: A. Kempf, G. Mangano, R. B. Mann
references.title: Hilbert space representation of the minimal length uncertainty relation
references.significance: Canonical formalism for GUP and minimal length.

references.title: String Theory and Noncommutative Geometry
references.significance: Establishes the string-theory bridge to Moyal NCG and the Seiberg-Witten map.

references.title: Doubly-Special Relativity: facts, myths and some key open issues
references.significance: Authoritative review of DSR and its relation to kappa-Poincare.

references.authors: A. H. Chamseddine, A. Connes, M. Marcolli
references.title: Gravity and the standard model with neutrino mixing
references.significance: Full Standard Model with neutrino mixing coupled to gravity from NCG.

references.title: On the spectral characterization of manifolds
references.significance: Reconstruction theorem: commutative spectral triples are exactly Riemannian spin manifolds.

references.authors: J. Aastrup, J. M. Grimstrup, R. Nest
references.title: On Spectral Triples in Quantum Gravity I
references.significance: Builds a spectral triple over the space of connections, bridging NCG and loop quantum gravity.

references.title: Resilience of the Spectral Standard Model
references.significance: Sigma scalar field restores consistency of the Higgs-mass prediction with 125 GeV.

references.authors: A. H. Chamseddine, A. Connes, W. D. van Suijlekom
references.title: Inner Fluctuations in NCG without the first order condition
references.significance: Relaxes the first-order condition, enabling Pati-Salam and beyond-SM models.

references.authors: A. H. Chamseddine, A. Connes, V. Mukhanov
references.title: Quanta of Geometry: Noncommutative Aspects
references.significance: Volume quantization from higher Heisenberg relation; forces SM algebras.

references.authors: A. H. Chamseddine, A. Connes, V. Mukhanov
references.title: Geometry and the Quantum: Basics
references.significance: Detailed derivation of M_2(H) and M_4(C) from the quantization condition.

references.authors: A. H. Chamseddine, A. Connes, W. D. van Suijlekom
references.title: Grand Unification in the Spectral Pati-Salam Model
references.significance: Pati-Salam unification with gauge-coupling unification from spectral geometry.

references.title: Monte Carlo simulations of random non-commutative geometries
references.significance: Launches random NCG / Dirac ensembles as a path-integral approach to quantum gravity.

references.authors: F. D'Andrea, M. A. Kurkov, F. Lizzi
references.title: Wick Rotation and Fermion Doubling in Noncommutative Geometry
references.significance: Analyses the Euclidean-to-Lorentzian problem and fermion doubling.

references.title: Noncommutative Geometry and Particle Physics
references.significance: Pedagogical review of the spectral Standard Model and Higgs predictions.

references.authors: M. Marcolli, E. Pierpaoli, K. Teh
references.title: The coupling of topology and inflation in Noncommutative Cosmology
references.significance: Spectral cosmology: inflation, cosmic topology and variable cosmological constant.

references.authors: H. Hessam, M. Khalkhali, N. Pagliaroli
references.title: Double scaling limits of Dirac ensembles and Liouville quantum gravity
references.significance: Connects Dirac ensembles to Liouville quantum gravity and 2D gravity.

references.title: Coloured combinatorial maps and quartic bi-tracial 2-matrix ensembles from noncommutative geometry
references.significance: Links random NCG to multimatrix models and map enumeration.

references.title: Noncommutative Geometry and Particle Physics (2nd ed.)
references.significance: Standard Open-Access textbook; includes BSM models and noncommutative quantum theory.

references.title: Hearing the Shape of the Universe: A Personal Journey in Noncommutative Geometry
references.significance: 94-page 2025 survey of the entire NCG-to-physics program; current state and outlook.

references.title: Spectral torsion of the internal noncommutative geometry of the Standard Model
references.significance: Computes spectral torsion functional linking Yukawa and Majorana data to geometry.

references.title: Symmetry Breaking and Phase Transitions in Random Non-Commutative Geometries and Related Random-Matrix Ensembles
references.significance: Maps phase transitions and symmetry breaking in the large-N limit of Dirac ensembles.

references.title: Bootstrapping Noncommutative Geometry with Dirac Ensembles
references.significance: Applies bootstrap methods to constrain Dirac ensemble observables.

references.authors: J. Gamble, M. Khalkhali, N. Pagliaroli
references.title: The Schwinger-Dyson equations for random fuzzy geometries coupled to matter
references.significance: Derives Schwinger-Dyson equations for matter-coupled fuzzy geometries.

references.title: Dimension and Dimensional Reduction in Quantum Gravity
references.significance: Reviews UV dimensional reduction shared by NCG, asymptotic safety, CDT, LQG.

references.title: Emergent Geometry and Gravity from Matrix Models: an Introduction
references.significance: Shows effective Riemannian geometry and gravity emerging from fuzzy/matrix models.

references.authors: J. Kudler-Flam, S. Leutheusser, G. Satishchandran
references.title: Generalized Black Hole Entropy is von Neumann Entropy
references.significance: Uses Connes' von Neumann algebra classification (type II/III) to identify generalized entropy holographically.

references.authors: E. Valero, H. Gisbert, V. Ilisie
references.title: The Generalized Uncertainty Principle. New Bounds and Trends
references.significance: Recent effective-field-theory bounds on the GUP/minimal-length parameter from experimental data.

openProblems.statement: Formulate the spectral action and spectral triples in a Lorentzian signature and quantize the resulting gravitational theory, controlling Wick rotation and fermion doubling.
openProblems.whyHard: Noncompactness and indefinite signature destroy the compact resolvent and discrete real spectrum of D on which the entire formalism rests; a fully Lorentzian definition of a spectral triple is still incomplete.
openProblems.attempts: Krein-space / Lorentzian spectral triples; D'Andrea-Kurkov-Lizzi analysis of Wick rotation and fermion doubling (2016); ongoing pseudo-Riemannian generalizations.

openProblems.statement: Turn noncommutative geometry into a genuine quantum theory of gravity, not merely a classical/effective action, with a controlled continuum limit reconstructing 4D spacetime.
openProblems.whyHard: The space of Dirac operators has no canonical measure; the spectral action is classical; random-NCG path integrals are controlled only for finite/fuzzy geometries and the N->infinity limit is not generically tractable.
openProblems.attempts: Random NCG / Dirac ensembles (Barrett-Glaser); double-scaling limits and links to Liouville gravity (Hessam-Khalkhali-Pagliaroli 2022); bootstrap and Schwinger-Dyson methods (2025-2026).

openProblems.statement: Derive the finite Standard Model algebra C + H + M_3(C) from a deeper principle rather than postulated axioms, and explain why exactly this algebra.
openProblems.whyHard: Classification arguments and volume quantization strongly prefer it, but relaxing the first-order condition opens Pati-Salam and other options, so uniqueness is conditional on axiom choices that are themselves unexplained.
openProblems.attempts: Classification of irreducible almost-commutative geometries (Chamseddine-Connes); quanta-of-geometry derivation of M_2(H), M_4(C) (2014); Pati-Salam analysis (2015).

openProblems.statement: Predict fermion masses and CKM/PMNS mixing angles from the geometry instead of feeding them in as parameters of the finite Dirac operator.
openProblems.whyHard: Geometry constrains the structure (representations, hypercharges, gauge-coupling relations) but leaves the Yukawa matrices as free input data; no dynamical principle currently fixes their entries.
openProblems.attempts: Spectral torsion functionals linking Yukawa/Majorana data to geometric invariants (2025); moduli-space analyses of finite Dirac operators.

openProblems.statement: Clarify the precise relationship between Connes' spectral (Riemannian, compact spin) program and the deformed-spacetime branch (Moyal, kappa-Minkowski, DSR).
openProblems.whyHard: Lorentzian signature and Hopf-algebra symmetries of kappa-Minkowski do not fit the standard spectral-triple axioms; whether kappa-Minkowski admits a genuine spectral triple is only partially resolved.
openProblems.attempts: Twisted spectral triples; spectral triples on kappa-Minkowski and on the Moyal plane; Lizzi and collaborators' bridging work.

openProblems.statement: Find a falsifiable, currently accessible prediction that distinguishes noncommutative geometry from other approaches.
openProblems.whyHard: Characteristic effects (Lorentz violation, minimal length, modified dispersion) are Planck-suppressed (~E/E_Planck); spectral-SM predictions are largely post-dictive or at inaccessible scales.
openProblems.attempts: Lorentz-violation bounds from gamma-ray bursts (M > 10^15-10^17 GeV); GUP laboratory bounds on beta; energy-resolved GRB polarimetry (2025).

openProblems.statement: Establish a dictionary between the phases of random Dirac ensembles (matrix integrals) and continuum geometric/topological data, and tame sign/convergence problems.
openProblems.whyHard: Matrix integrals over Dirac operators can have sign and convergence issues, and the physical meaning of observed phase transitions relative to geometric reconstruction is not understood.
openProblems.attempts: Monte Carlo phase studies (Barrett-Glaser, Glaser); analytic phase-transition results (Hessam et al. 2026); topological-recursion and bi-tracial matrix-model techniques (2023-2026).

connections.description: Effective theory of D-branes in a constant NS-NS B-field lives on a Moyal noncommutative space with theta ~ B^{-1}; the Seiberg-Witten map gives an explicit equivalence of commutative and noncommutative gauge descriptions. IKKT/BFSS matrix models produce fuzzy spaces and noncommutative gauge theories. Strongest and best-documented bridge.

Aastrup-Grimstrup-Nest construct a semifinite spectral triple over the space of connections whose holonomy-loop algebra and Dirac operator reproduce (up to a symmetry group) the diffeomorphism-invariant Hilbert space of LQG, with [D,.] encoding the Poisson bracket of GR (Quantum Holonomy Theory). Technically demanding; adopted only at the margins of the LQG community.

UV dimensional reduction (spectral dimension running to ~2) appears in deformed/Moyal NCG just as in the asymptotic-safety fixed-point regime; whether noncommutativity is a manifestation of a UV fixed point is open and explicitly little studied.

Both encode fundamental spacetime discreteness/minimal length and non-pointlike structure. Their spectral-dimension behaviours are probe-dependent and partially complementary: (a) In NCG/deformed Laplacians the spectral dimension decreases toward ~2 in the UV (heat-kernel / diffusion probe on the deformed space). (b) In causal sets, the random-walk probe on the discrete causal order gives an INCREASING d_s at short scales (Eichhorn-Mizera, arXiv:1311.2530), while the nonlocal d'Alembertian probe gives a DECREASING d_s to ~2 universally (Belenchia et al., arXiv:1507.00330). The claim that the dimension runs 'oppositely' — NCG down, CST up — is therefore only half correct: it applies to the random-walk probe in CST versus NCG heat-kernel; the d'Alembertian probe in CST and NCG heat-kernel agree on reduction to ~2. Direct technical bridges remain essentially absent.

Connes' classification of von Neumann factors (local QFT = type III_1) is central to the recent crossed-product algebra approach to holographic observables, where generalized black-hole entropy equals von Neumann entropy of a type-II algebra. NCG supplies the operator-algebraic language; explicit use of Connes' spectral formalism is still limited but rapidly growing.

Spacetime noncommutativity regularizes singularities and quantizes horizon area in units of a minimal area, resonating with Bekenstein-Hawking entropy; noncommutative-inspired black holes have regular cores. Link to the information paradox is indirect.

In IKKT-type matrix models and fuzzy spaces, effective Riemannian geometry and gravity emerge from the noncommutative algebra and Dirac-operator fluctuations; random NCG (Dirac ensembles) likewise lets geometry emerge from matrix degrees of freedom. Bridge to entropic/Verlinde emergence is weaker.

Both random NCG (Dirac ensembles) and GFT/tensor models use matrix/tensor degrees of freedom from which geometry should emerge; the shared combinatorial-map and multimatrix-integral structure is striking, but an explicit identification (are Dirac ensembles a special GFT?) is missing - a prime candidate for an undiscovered bridge.

Common algebraic-geometric description of spacetime and use of deformations (quantum groups, Hopf algebras). Direct bridges between Connes' NCG and the twistor/amplitude program are almost nonexistent - an open, speculative frontier.

Noncommutative spacetimes and DSR predict energy-dependent photon speed, GRB time delays and modified dispersion relations; current Lorentz-violation bounds give M > 10^15-10^17 GeV and GUP gives laboratory bounds on beta. Concrete phenomenological program but no positive signal yet.

A spectral/supersymmetric version of the spectral action ('toward a supergravity spectral action') has been attempted, seeking to encode supergravity in spectral data; the construction is incomplete and the bridge largely conjectural.

The nonperturbative spectral action yields cosmological models: slow-roll inflation potentials sensitive to cosmic topology, variable cosmological constant, and constraints on early-universe geometry (Marcolli-Pierpaoli). A developed but niche bridge.

Connes' Tomita-Takesaki modular theory and the type-III_1 classification of von Neumann factors for local QFT (a core NCG result) underlie the modular/crossed-product algebra reformulations of the generalized entropy and the gravitational entanglement-of-spacetime program (2023+). The crossed product by the modular flow turns a type-III_1 algebra into type II, making a trace and hence a von Neumann entropy well defined, which equals the generalized entropy as G->0. NCG supplies the operator-algebraic backbone but its specific spectral-triple machinery is not yet explicitly used here.

Both programs make sharp statements about which low-energy effective field theories are admissible: the spectral action 'almost uniquely' selects the SM finite algebra C+H+M3(C) and its representation content, an algebraic analogue of swampland selection criteria from string theory. No explicit dictionary exists between NCG geometric constraints (KO-dimension, order-one condition, Poincare duality) and swampland conjectures (distance, weak gravity); a barely-touched but conceptually parallel selection-principle bridge.

Random NCG / Dirac ensembles realize a path integral over finite (fuzzy) geometries that is conceptually parallel to CDT's sum over triangulations, and both exhibit phase transitions and a dynamically reduced spectral dimension near 2 in the UV (Carlip's dimensional-reduction survey). Double-scaling limits of Dirac ensembles connect to Liouville quantum gravity / 2D gravity, the same minimal-model universality classes that appear in dynamical triangulations; an explicit map between the two discrete path integrals is missing.

The Lambda^0 term of the spectral action Tr f(D/Lambda) IS the conformal/trace anomaly: in d=4 the integrated Seeley-DeWitt coefficient a4(D^2) equals (1/16pi^2) int sqrt(g)(c W^2 - a E_4), so the C^2 (Weyl-squared) and Euler (Gauss-Bonnet) coefficients of the spectral action are literally the conformal-anomaly charges. The coefficient ratio is alpha_0/tau_0 = (-3 f_0/10pi^2)/(11 f_0/60pi^2) = -18/11 exactly, identical to c/(-a) = (1/40)/(-11/720) = -18/11 for a single Weyl fermion. Proven, scheme-independent, index-protected (Atiyah-Singer A-hat sector). Evidence: F-003 (exact, zero mismatch, sympy), F-014 (index protection, Duff Table 1 cross-check), draft-02 sec.2 (Gilkey/Vassilevich heat-kernel steps 1-5). Convention refs: Chamseddine-Connes hep-th/9606001, Vassilevich hep-th/0306138, Duff arXiv:2003.02688.

The conformal central charges (a,c) ARE the a4(D^2) coefficients of the Euler density E_4 and Weyl-squared W^2 read off from the spectral action's Lambda^0 term; they are not an independent input. For one Weyl fermion (a,c)=(11/720,1/40), and content-independence makes c/(-a)=-18/11 hold for 1, 45 (SM without nu_R) or 48 (with nu_R) fermions with zero residue. Evidence: F-003, F-014; lib/toe/ncg.py (central_charges, a4_ratio, spectral_action_ratio). Convention refs: Chamseddine-Connes hep-th/9606001, Duff arXiv:2003.02688.

Pillar-level umbrella over the spectral-action<->trace-anomaly and spectral-action<->central-charge identities: the NCG spectral-action cluster and the trace-anomaly cluster share one heat-kernel coefficient a4(D^2) of a single Dirac operator. The -18/11 fermionic ratio is the literal shared-math bridge that the project graph previously left undrawn (LOV-18-11-overlaps sec.1.4). Evidence: F-003, F-014, draft-02 sec.2.

Spectral dimension and the spectral action live off the same Dirac spectrum: spectral dimension reads the UV/IR scaling exponent of the return probability Tr e^{-sigma D^2}, while the spectral action reads the same Seeley-DeWitt coefficients a_0,a_2,a_4 of the same heat kernel. The existing heat-kernel-expansion -> spectral-dimension edge puts this bridge one node away; ML link-prediction ranks it the top NCG-touching candidate (link-predictions.json score ~0.896, ~7 shared neighbours). Quantitative -18/11<->d_s relation remains open (d_s is probe-dependent, F-001/F-002). Evidence: link-predictions.json (0.896), F-001/F-002.
